what_it_does: Defines a React `Sidebar` component: a left navigation aside built from a static NAV_SECTIONS data structure (grouped nav items with id/label/icon and optional shortcut/count/new-dot keys). Renders a brand wordmark with a collapse button, the grouped nav buttons (highlighting the active id, routing the search action separately), and a system-health footer. Exposes window.Sidebar.
observations: NAV_SECTIONS is a hardcoded array of 5 groups: Workspace, System, Foundations, Build with, AI & settings.
Each item has id/label/icon; some carry countKey (e.g. typeCountTotal, colorCount), newKey (inboxNew, needsAI), shortcut ('⌘K'), or isAction.
The '__search' item is an isAction that calls onOpenSearch instead of onSelect.
Icons rendered via a CvIcon component (not defined here) with tone='bronze' size=16; brand uses an wordmark at ../assets/logos/conceptv-wordmark-black.png.
Counts and new-dots are driven by a `counts` prop looked up by countKey/newKey.
Footer renders counts.healthScore% coverage and counts.gaps with singular/plural 'gap'/'gaps'.
One inline style object on the shortcut badge (font/color/background via CSS vars); most styling is via className.
Props: active, onSelect, counts, onOpenSearch, onCollapse — onCollapse/onOpenSearch optional-chained.
standouts: Navigation is fully data-driven from NAV_SECTIONS, so adding a nav entry is a data edit.
Single search item is special-cased as an action rather than a selectable view.
conventions: Window-global component pattern (window.Sidebar) consistent with the app.
Depends on external globals CvIcon and (in JSX) the broader DS conventions.
Uses CSS-var-based inline style for the shortcut badge rather than a class.
concerns: The shortcut badge uses a long inline style object instead of a className, a small inconsistency with the otherwise class-driven styling.
Hardcoded asset path to the wordmark image; no fallback if missing.
useEffect_sb is destructured at top but not used in the visible body.
notes: A data-driven left nav: edit NAV_SECTIONS to change navigation; counts/badges come from a counts prop and search is a special action item.
questions: Where are the countKey values (typeCountTotal, colorCount, etc.) populated and kept in sync with real data?
Why is useEffect_sb imported but not used here?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: header comment 'left nav of asset classes' aligns with the rendered grouped navigation.
apparent_themes: Design-system asset taxonomy (colors, type, icons, components, templates, voice)
intention_signals: Aims to be the spine of the design-system studio: one nav listing every asset class and system view, with live counts and a coverage/gaps health readout so the sidebar doubles as a completeness dashboard.
novelty: The nav mirrors a design-system's own taxonomy (Foundations / Build with / System) and folds a health-coverage metric into the chrome.
contribution: Contributes a config-driven sidebar whose item list encodes the studio's asset taxonomy plus count/new/health bindings that a fused system would keep as the navigation source.
summary_for_embedding: React Sidebar component rendering a left nav from a static NAV_SECTIONS taxonomy with active-state, count badges, new-dots, a search action, and a coverage/gaps health footer.
intention_for_embedding: To give the design-system studio a single data-driven navigation that lists every asset class and reflects coverage/health.
